design_philosophy.core_loop: Execute dual-joystick move inputs to land hits on opponent; first player to score two points wins. Repeat across progressively challenging rounds with new environmental backdrops.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision execution of martial arts techniques against CPU or player opponent; point-based scoring system creates win/loss clarity with no health bar obfuscation.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master of karate combat, progressing through tournament stages with varied global locations; execute technical moves to outmaneuver opponents.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deviates from health-bar convention—single successful hit ends round and awards points (full or half). No damage accumulation; matches decided by best-of-three point scoring. Emphasizes move precision over resource management.

difficulty.lives_system: Single loss ends game; no lives system documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: iOS remake included difficulty mode selector; original arcade/NES versions feature progressive difficulty scaling across rounds with new opponent locations and increased challenge per stage

progression.unlock_mechanism: Each round defeat advances to next location/stage with stronger opponent; Player vs Player edition cycles through 12 worldwide tournament locations before looping
replayability_hooks: Two-player versus mode for competitive play
12 distinct environmental backdrops in tournament progression
Bonus rounds between matches (evade potted plants, break boards, stop charging bull)
Numeric scoring for top-ten leaderboard tracking
Multiple difficulty settings in modern ports

world_building_techniques: Tournament progression across 12 international locations (harbor, Mount Fuji garden, bamboo forest, desert with tipis, dojo, forest path, etc.)
Context of competing 'for girls' in Player vs Player edition
Digitized Japanese speech callouts from referee (judge announces 'Half point' or 'Full point')
Visual differentiation via opponent gi color (white vs red) and environmental backdrops

faction_indicators: Karate gi colors (white vs red) distinguish player one from player two/CPU
Tournament structure implies martial arts school or dojo affiliation
danger_signals: Opponent presence escalates difficulty per round
Progressive stage hazards in bonus rounds (projectiles, boards, charging bull)
environmental_storytelling: 12 global tournament locations establish international martial arts competition narrative
Dojo setting as traditional training ground
Varied architecture reflects different cultures (Mount Fuji, windmills, tipis, Japanese garden)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Karate Champ NES/FDS)
